author: thomas
body: cwmem's retrieval stack has three layers: (1) SQLite FTS5 BM25 lexical search over title, body, and tags; (2) Model2Vec semantic similarity using vendored 384-dim embeddings with cosine distance; (3) optional graph expansion via BFS traversal of edges. Hybrid mode merges lexical and semantic candidate sets using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with k=60 (Cormack et al. 2009). Each search hit includes an explanation object with lexical_rank, semantic_rank, rrf_score, and matched_fields. Users can force --lexical-only or --semantic-only modes. Graph expansion adds related resources at configurable depth (1-4). Filters (tag, type, author, date range) apply at the query level before fusion.
title: Hybrid search architecture: FTS5 + Model2Vec + RRF

author: thomas
body: Every cwmem CLI command emits exactly one JSON envelope to stdout with a stable schema: {schema_version, request_id, ok, command, target, result, warnings, errors, metrics}. This machine-first design enables reliable piping to jq, programmatic consumption by AI agents, and consistent error handling. Errors include code, message, retryable flag, suggested_action, and details. Warnings use code+message+details. Exit codes map to error categories: 0=success, 10=validation, 20=auth, 40=conflict, 50=io, 90=internal. Planned but unimplemented: table and markdown output modes via --format flag.
title: Single JSON envelope output contract

author: thomas
body: cwmem splits state into runtime (.cwmem/) and tracked (memory/) directories. The sync export produces deterministic, git-friendly artifacts: per-entry markdown with YAML frontmatter, JSONL for all resource types, taxonomy seed JSON, and a SHA256 manifest. Same database state always produces identical bytes. The --check flag detects drift between exported artifacts and current DB state without writing. This enables CI gates: 'cwmem sync export --check' fails if artifacts are stale. The sync import workflow rebuilds the runtime database from tracked artifacts, enabling new clones to bootstrap from committed state.
title: Deterministic sync export for version control

author: thomas
body: All mutating operations serialize through an exclusive sidecar lock file at .cwmem/memory.sqlite.lock, implemented via portalocker (preferred) with fcntl/msvcrt fallbacks. The lock is non-blocking by default (--wait-lock 0); callers can specify a timeout in seconds. Lock metadata (PID, hostname, command, request_id, acquired_at) is written to a companion .json file for debugging stuck locks. Reads are lock-free and parallel-safe. This design was chosen over SQLite's built-in locking because sidecar files work across filesystems, are human-readable, and decouple lock semantics from SQLite pragma configuration.
title: Sidecar lock file for write serialization

author: thomas
body: SHA256 fingerprints are computed from canonical JSON of content fields (excluding timestamps). They serve two purposes: (1) Optimistic concurrency control via --expected-fingerprint on update, which rejects stale writes with ERR_CONFLICT_STALE_FINGERPRINT; (2) Rebuild optimization for embeddings and FTS, where content_fingerprint + model_version comparisons skip re-encoding unchanged rows. This avoids expensive full rebuilds on incremental changes. Fingerprints are prefixed with 'sha256:' for self-describing verification.
title: Fingerprint-based optimistic concurrency and rebuild optimization

author: thomas
body: High-risk operations (sync export/import) support a reviewable plan workflow: (1) 'cwmem plan sync-export' generates a PlanArtifact JSON capturing workflow, request_hash, options, summary, and impacted resources; (2) Human or agent reviews the plan; (3) 'cwmem validate --plan FILE' checks the plan against current state via request_hash comparison; (4) 'cwmem apply --plan FILE' executes with drift protection. Additionally, --dry-run copies the database to a temp directory and runs mutations on the copy, returning previews without side effects. --idempotency-key enables safe retries by recording (key, command_id) -> response pairs.
title: Plan/validate/apply safety workflow

author: thomas
body: cwmem seeds three taxonomy files on init: tags.json, entity-types.json, and relation-types.json under memory/taxonomy/. These provide governed vocabularies for enterprise architecture work. Enforcement is tiered: entity types emit warnings at creation time but don't block; edge relation types are validated but not blocked; 'cwmem verify' runs comprehensive validation across all resources. Taxonomies are committed to git, making vocabulary evolution visible in code review. Default seeds include EA-domain terms (capability, domain, initiative, system, team) and relationship types (depends_on, supersedes, references, owned_by, etc.).
title: Taxonomy system for governed vocabularies

author: thomas
body: The cwmem data model has four core resource types stored in SQLite (schema v3): (1) Entries (mem-NNNNNN) — titled knowledge records with body, type, status, author, tags, metadata, provenance, related_ids, and entity_refs. Support CRUD with fingerprint-based concurrency. (2) Events (evt-NNNNNN) — append-only log records with event_type, body, occurred_at, and resource references. Lifecycle events auto-generated on entry create/update. (3) Entities (ent-NNNNNN) — knowledge graph nodes with entity_type, name, description, aliases, and status. (4) Edges (edg-NNNNNN) — directed relationships between any resource pair with relation_type, confidence (0-1), provenance, and is_inferred flag. Self-links are rejected. Duplicate edges (same source/target/relation) are blocked. All resources use Crockford Base32 internal IDs and sequential public IDs.
title: Data model: entries, events, entities, edges

author: thomas
body: cwmem is organized under src/cwmem/ with three packages: (1) cli/ — Typer command definitions split by concern: setup.py (init, status, guide + command catalog builder), read.py (get, list, search, related, log, stats), write.py (add, update, tag-add/remove, event-add, entity-add, link, deprecate), graph.py (graph visualization), sync.py (export, import), maintenance.py (build, plan, validate, apply, verify). Commands registered via per-module register(app) functions called from __main__.py. (2) core/ — Business logic: store.py (SQLite CRUD), models.py (50+ Pydantic models), paths.py (directory structure), ids.py (Crockford Base32 + sequential IDs), fingerprints.py (SHA256), events.py (append-only log), fts.py (FTS5), embeddings.py (Model2Vec), hybrid_search.py (RRF fusion), graph.py (entity/edge ops), export.py (deterministic rendering), importer.py (snapshot loading), planner.py (plan generation), validator.py (state validation), safety.py (dry-run/idempotency), locking.py (portalocker), automation.py (future hooks). (3) output/ — envelope.py (JSON envelope builder, exit codes, error types, contextvars for request tracking), json.py (orjson serialization), table.py (planned).
title: Source code architecture: cli / core / output modules

author: thomas
body: Two GitHub Actions workflows run on push to master: (1) CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) — Python 3.13, uv sync --frozen, ruff check, pyright type checking, pytest, uv build. (2) Publish (.github/workflows/publish.yml) — builds wheel+sdist, uploads artifacts, then publishes to PyPI using Trusted Publisher OIDC (no API tokens). The publish job uses pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish with skip-existing:true. Local quality gate mirrors CI: ruff check, pyright, pytest, uv build. Version is defined in src/cwmem/__init__.py and read by hatch via [tool.hatch.version] path config.
title: CI/CD pipeline: GitHub Actions with PyPI OIDC publishing

author: thomas
body: The test suite uses pytest with a subprocess-based CLI integration pattern. The run_cli fixture spawns 'python -m cwmem' in a tmp_path, captures stdout/stderr, and returns CompletedProcess. Helper functions parse_envelope_any_exit() and run_ok() validate JSON envelope structure. Tests are organized by feature: test_entry_crud.py (CRUD + fingerprint tracking), test_events.py (append-only log + lifecycle), test_fts.py (FTS5 indexing), test_hybrid_search.py (lexical + semantic + RRF), test_embeddings.py (Model2Vec pipeline), test_locking.py (lock acquisition + timeout), test_idempotency.py (replay + hash matching), test_dry_run.py (preview without side effects), test_graph_cli.py (entity/edge CRUD + traversal), test_sync_export.py + test_sync_import.py + test_round_trip.py (export/import cycle), test_plan_apply.py (plan workflow), test_verify.py (verification), test_validation_errors.py (input validation + error codes), test_help_cli.py (help text + command discovery), test_guide.py (guide document schema). Phase helpers provide test data builders for progressive feature development.
title: Test suite: 82 tests across 20 modules with CLI integration pattern

author: thomas
body: The embedding pipeline in core/embeddings.py loads a vendored Model2Vec model from models/model2vec/ (manifest.json + safetensors weights). On 'cwmem build', it reads all entries (title+body concatenated) and events (body), encodes them to 384-dim float32 vectors, normalizes if manifest.normalize=true, and stores as raw bytes in the embeddings table. Fingerprint-based optimization: each embedding row tracks content_fingerprint (SHA256 of text) and model_version; if both match, the row is skipped during rebuild. This makes incremental rebuilds fast — only changed or new entries are re-encoded. The model loads lazily and is cached for the duration of a build session. Query encoding uses the same model for cosine similarity computation during semantic search.
title: Embedding pipeline: vendored Model2Vec with fingerprint-based incremental rebuild
